stimulus: Miriam Makeba (1932–2008), also known as "Mama Africa," was an internationally celebrated South African singer and activist. Her recordings ______blank the rhythms and languages of southern Africa to audiences around the world.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested is punctuation use between a verb and an object. No punctuation is needed between the verb "introduced" and its object "the rhythms and languages of southern Africa to audiences around the world." The object helps complete the idea of the verb, and any punctuation between the two results in an ungrammatical sentence.

Choice A is incorrect because no punctuation is needed between the verb and its object. Choice B is incorrect because no punctuation is needed between the verb and its object. Choice D is incorrect because no punctuation is needed between the verb and its object.
